channel.title: Health
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Health, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Influencers are promoting peptides for better health. What's the science say?
item.description: The latest wellness craze involves injecting these molecules for athletic performance, longevity and more. Scientists say the research isn't keeping p...

item.title: Still Skipping Oatmeal? Here's Why You Might Want to Reconsider
item.description: How a simple bowl of oatmeal can support your cholesterol

item.title: If Intermittent Fasting Isn't Working for You, Science Says You're Not Alone
item.description: A new study says intermittent fasting isn't the weight-loss hack we thought

item.title: Families call for increasing access to early diagnoses and interventions for autism
item.description: Families and autism experts say that the federal government could do a lot more to support autistic children by increasing access to early diagnosis a...

item.title: PHOTOS: Your car has a lot to say about who you are
item.description: Photographer Martin Roemer visited 22 countries — from the U.S. to Senegal to India — to show how our identities are connected to our mode of transpor...

item.title: Looking for life purpose? Start with building social ties
item.description: Research shows that having a sense of purpose can lower stress levels and boost our mental health. Finding meaning may not have to be an ambitious pro...

item.title: What Is Coconut Sugar? Here's The Deal On This Cane Sugar Alternative
item.description: If you're eager to sample some other sweet options besides cane sugar, coconut sugar may be a healthy alternative.

item.title: Watching This On TV May Actually Boost Your Well-Being, Study Finds
A Neurologist-Approved Trick For Using Technology More Mindfully
item.description: A simple suggestion to "reduce your screen time" might not be possible (or effective) for everyone.

item.title: A huge study finds a link between cannabis use in teens and psychosis later
item.description: Researchers followed more than 400,000 teens until they were adults. It found that those who used marijuana were more likely to develop serious mental...

item.title: Tips for Making Exercise a Daily Habit
What Happens When Blood Sugar Falls Too Low
7 Nutrition Red Flags to Know About
item.description: Feeling unusually tired or noticing subtle changes in hair, nails, mood, or appetite? These signs may indicate nutrition gaps worth discussing with yo...
